file_insights.purpose: Application bootstrap orchestration and startup sequence management

patterns_discovered.description: Bootstrap orchestrated through 7 sequential phases: environment → database → cache → container → modules → server → ready
Centralized shutdown handler with reverse dependency order deactivation and signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT, uncaughtException, unhandledRejection)
After each external service connection, health checks performed to validate successful connection before proceeding
Module-scoped state management for current bootstrap phase, service connectivity status, and timing information
Bootstrap options allow skipping database, cache, and modules independently for testing scenarios
Centralized registration of SIGTERM, SIGINT, uncaughtException, and unhandledRejection handlers with graceful shutdown
Console logging at each phase with ISO timestamps and structured prefixes for observability

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Skip flags don't prevent service initialization in container
gotchas_discovered.details: Even when skipDatabase/skipCache is true, registerCoreServices() still registers Prisma and Redis instances; skipping only prevents connection attempts
Even with skipModules=true, a ModuleLoader instance is created and registered with DI container, though it won't discover/load actual modules
Signal handlers registered only after all bootstrap phases complete; any error before handler registration will not trigger graceful shutdown
Core modules (identified by manifest.core flag) are intentionally skipped during deactivation, assuming they clean up last

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Multiple shutdown calls prevented but only after logging
gotchas_discovered.details: Subsequent shutdown calls return early after logging, but this occurs after the isShuttingDown flag is set, potentially causing race conditions

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Module activation errors logged but don't halt startup
gotchas_discovered.details: Individual module activation failures are caught and logged but don't prevent bootstrap from completing or returning ready status

approach_outcome.summary: Complete bootstrap sequence implementation for E-Menum application
key_achievements: Implemented 7-phase startup orchestration covering environment, database, cache, DI container, and module loading
Created comprehensive graceful shutdown system with signal handling and reverse-order module deactivation
Built flexible configuration system allowing selective service initialization for testing
Integrated health checks for database and Redis connectivity
Established detailed logging and status tracking throughout bootstrap lifecycle
Designed type-safe interfaces for bootstrap status, options, phases, and results

recommendations.recommendation: Add timeout configuration to bootstrap phases to prevent indefinite hangs on unresponsive services
Register signal handlers before bootstrap starts to catch errors during initialization, not just after completion
Add structured logging (pino/winston) integration instead of console.log for production environments with log level control
Consider using async locks or a state machine library to prevent race conditions in concurrent shutdown scenarios
Add comprehensive error scenario tests (DB connection failure, Redis timeout, module load errors) to ensure graceful degradation
Validate that all critical modules are loaded and activated before marking system as 'ready'; currently only logs errors without blocking
Export startup timing metrics and phase durations for performance monitoring and bottleneck identification
